rationale: Core actors and resource lifecycles must be surfaced from scattered input.
Explicit launch requirements must not be deferred as scope creep.
Excluded/unapproved items must be explicitly deferred not pulled into MVP.
Decision/approval lifecycle must be modeled as first-class not conflated with comments.
Access-policy source-of-truth question must be surfaced as blocking.
Narrow offline requirement must not be escalated to full offline-first.
Feature decomposition that duplicates cross-cutting contracts must be flagged.
Technology choices must match team capacity and operational constraints.
EU residency vs global support/analytics topology contradiction must be surfaced.
Shared foundations must be sequenced before dependent downstream specs.
Platform team bottleneck must be identified and addressed in P0 or escalated.
Justified horizontal P0 must not be rejected by vertical-slice default.
Latency vs residency vs staffing trilemma must be surfaced as tradeoff checkpoint.
Realtime as infrastructure (not domain) is correct when team explicitly declines product ownership.
Structurally unresolved access/lifecycle model with no owner must be marked BLOCKING.
